title: Wirtschaft in Deutschland: Industrie erreicht Rekordniveau bei Aufträgen
summary: Die deutsche Wirtschaft hat so viele offene Bestellungen wie noch nie seit Beginn der Erfassung im Jahr 2015. Besonders legten die Aufträge in der Elektro- und Fahrzeugindustrie zu. Trotzdem könnten Jobs weiter gefährdet sein.

title: USA: EU setzt Null-Zoll-Deal von Donald Trump unter Zeitdruck um
summary: Bis 4. Juli muss die EU den Handelsdeal mit Donald Trump umsetzen, sonst droht der US-Präsident mit hohen Autozöllen. Nun haben sich das Europaparlament und die Mitgliedstaaten auf die Details geeinigt. Doch zwei Hürden bleiben.

title: EU stimmt Zolldeal mit USA zu
summary: Die Europäische Union hat die vollständige Umsetzung des umstrittenen Zolldeals mit den USA beschlossen. Zölle auf US-Industriewaren werden damit abgeschafft – die USA erheben hingegen bis zu 15 Prozent auf EU-Produkte.

title: Südkorea: Starbucks ruft »Panzertag« aus – an Gedenktag von Militärverbrechen

title: Zahlreiche Durchsuchungen : Wissenschaftsspionage für China? Zwei Verdächtige gefasst
summary: Die Bundesanwaltschaft lässt in München zwei mutmaßliche chinesische Spione festnehmen. Das Ehepaar mit deutscher Staatsangehörigkeit soll für einen chinesischen Geheimdienst Kontakt zu Wissenschaftlern an deutschen Hochschulen aufgenommen haben.

title: "Wahnprojekt" für Minister-Egos : Teurer, kürzer, langsamer: Schnellzug-Strecke wird zum Desaster für London

title: Target Delivers Biggest Sales Gain in Four Years
summary: Target posted its best comparable sales growth in four years and boosted its outlook as the retailer’s turnaround bid gains traction. The chain also raised its annual revenue guidance by 2 percentage points to about 4%. Jen Bartashus of Bloomberg Intelligence has more. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Stocks Rebound Before Nvidia as Bonds Find Respite: Markets Wrap
summary: Stocks found some footing as the selloff in bonds eased and traders awaited earnings from Nvidia Corp., the world’s most valuable company and a linchpin of the artificial-intelligence trade.
content: Cameron Dawson, CIO at NewEdge Wealth, sees the pullback in tech stocks as “perfectly normal and perfectly prudent,” but warns that “a simple correction in those names back to trend could be damaging for the overall market-cap weighted index.”

title: Why is China's Xi Hosting Russia's Putin After Trump Visit? | The Pulse 5/20
summary:
"The Pulse With Francine Lacqua" is all about conversations with high profile guests in the beating heart of global business, economics, finance and politics. Based in London, we go wherever the story is, bringing you exclusive interviews and market-moving scoops.

Today's guests:
Bhanu Baweja, UBS Investment Bank, Chief Strategist; Pierre Wunsch, National Bank of Belgium, Governor; Sebastian Mallaby, Council on Foreign Relations, Senior Fellow; Jed Laskowitz, JPMorgan Asset Management, Private Markets and Customized Solutions Global Head.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Europe’s Biggest Gas Market Isn’t Setting Aside Enough Winter Fuel
summary: Germany’s vast storage sites are less than 30% full, and that’s a concern as the Iran war continues to squeeze supplies.
content: WILHELMSHAVEN, GERMANY - FEBRUARY 28: The Hoegh Esperanza FSRU ship floats coupled to the Plaquemine LNG (liquified natural gas) tanker at the LNG terminal behind the Wilhelmshaven petroleum and natural gas storage facility on February 28, 2025 near Wilhelmshaven, Germany. The Wilhelmshaven LNG terminal, built by the German government following Russia's invasion of Ukraine and the halt of Russian natural gas imports to Germany, is the main terminal for natural gas imports from the United States. U.S. President Donald Trump announced recently that he plans to impose 25% tariffs on a variety of imports from Europe soon. (Photo by Sean Gallup/Getty Images) Photographer: Sean Gallup/Getty Images Europe

title: US Consumer Slowdown Threatens Stocks Rally, UBS Says
summary: Bhanu Baweja, chief investment strategist at UBS Investment Bank, tells Bloomberg Television that the equity markets rally is at risk from a slowdown in consumer spending as real disposable income growth nears zero and fiscal support fades. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Value Stocks With Earnings Strength Post 3,500% Run Since 2000
summary: Turns out, loading up on technology giants isn’t the only route to better returns. Value companies, too, stand a decent chance of trouncing the market — as long as several conditions are met.
content: Traders work on the floor of the New York Stock Exchange (NYSE) in New York, US, on Wednesday, May 13, 2026. GMR Solutions Inc., an air and ground emergency medical services company that counts KKR & Co. among its backers, raised $479 million in a downsized IPO after revising its target earlier on Tuesday.
